rationale: The cited paper is discussed in depth in the literature review and again in the latent-class section, not just listed in a citation cluster. The citing paper uses Mazumdar and Papatla (2000) as a direct comparison point for studying reference-price shopper heterogeneity, then argues that its latent-class setup is exploratory and does not permit statistically verifiable correlation-based profiling. The citing paper positions its own continuous correlated heterogeneity model and cross-category correlation tests as methodological improvements over that prior approach.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on and qualifies Mazumdar and Papatla (2000) by arguing that latent-class IRP/ERP segmentation is limited for inference about shopper profiles. It refines that line of work by introducing a continuous multivariate framework that estimates within- and cross-category correlations and enables statistical tests of profiling relationships.
